meta.title: Threading Ecology: A Coherence-Based Framework for Ecosystem Dynamics with Disturbance Cycling
meta.author: Zelenka_D_D

links.zenodo_record: Zelenka, D. D. (2025). Threading Ecology: A Coherence-Based Framework for Ecosystem Dynamics with Disturbance Cycling. Zenodo.

abstract: We propose a reconceptualization of ecological systems based on coherence dynamics and threading cycles[cite: 1]. The framework replaces energy-flow models with a Coherence Web where organisms function as memory activators and beauty gradient navigators within natural disturbance cycles[cite: 2, 3]. By integrating information theory and dynamical systems, we model ecosystems as threaded networks of memory and agency that undergo predictable threading-disturbance-redistribution phases[cite: 3, 4].

axioms.Coherence Rate: c = ΔΦ / Δτ; the rate of local system configuration change relative to threading depth[cite: 15].
axioms.Memory-Energy Relationship: E = Mc²; linking stored ecological information (memory density) to energetic potential[cite: 17].
axioms.Memory State Duality: M = M_active + M_latent; total memory consists of expressed interactions and dormant potential (e.g., seed banks)[cite: 19, 20].
axioms.Agency-driven Activation: M_latent + A → M_active; agency activates dormant ecological memory into expressed structure[cite: 21].
axioms.Beauty Gradient: B = ∇C; the directional flow toward higher systemic coherence guiding structural evolution[cite: 25].

lemmas.Threading Cycle Phases: Ecosystems evolve through five phases: Creative Threading, Memory Concentration, Disturbance Trigger, Memory Redistribution, and Threading Reset [cite: 43-52].
lemmas.Functional Roles: Roles in the Coherence Web include Coherence Architects (organizers), Synchronizers (coordinators), Resonance Clusters (memory anchors), Flow Agents (facilitators), and Memory Seeders (potential activators) [cite: 68-85].
lemmas.Disturbance Emergence: A_disturbance = f(∇²M); disturbances are triggered by 'memory curvature,' or accumulated coherence tension, to redistribute memory[cite: 37, 47].
lemmas.Scale-Invariant Agency: Agency manifests self-similarly across microbial, macro-ecological, and conscious scales to manage memory[cite: 41].

proof_steps: Define reality as emergent coherent geometries organized through agency via the Fabric Framework[cite: 14].
Show that high diversity promotes threading interactions, increasing active memory and system coherence[cite: 32, 43].
Derive the Disturbance Trigger: as memory density gradients steepen, the probability of redistributive disturbance rises to prevent systemic lock-in[cite: 39, 48].
Quantify ecosystem health using Global Resonance (phase alignment) and Cycle Completeness (activation/redistribution balance)[cite: 92, 98].
Demonstrate dynamic stability when coherence oscillations are bounded and the memory activation/redistribution ratio (α/β) is approximately 1[cite: 100].
Validate the framework through recovery trajectories where post-perturbation coherence (C_new) often exceeds original levels via latent memory activation[cite: 102, 107].

expansion_instructions.Conservation: Focus on preserving complete threading-disturbance cycles and agency distribution rather than just static species protection [cite: 112-114].
expansion_instructions.Restoration: Prioritize the assessment of latent memory (M_latent) and the design of appropriate disturbance regimes to trigger memory activation cascades [cite: 117-119].
expansion_instructions.Management: Monitor 'memory curvature' (∇²M) to anticipate natural disturbances and ensure the Cycle Completeness metric (C_cycle) remains near 1.0 [cite: 120-121].
